summary: Browser loader fetches and executes arbitrary HTML/JS from mutable Bitbucket branch with anti-analysis gating
details: index.js fetches https://bitbucket.org/p2p-alt-public/p2p-emis/raw/main/GameWebSight from a mutable `main` branch (no commit pin) and pipes the response body directly into document.open()/document.write()/document.close(), granting the Bitbucket repo owner arbitrary code execution in the embedding page's origin. The fetch-and-execute is gated behind an anti-analysis check (requires human-like mousemove+click/keypress with a 1200ms delay) and the script also blocks F12, Ctrl+Shift+I/J/C, Ctrl+U, Ctrl+S, and right-click — evasion behaviors designed to hide the payload from automated scanners and analysts rather than serve any legitimate purpose. Any site embedding loader1 hands its origin to whoever controls the Bitbucket repo, and the payload can be swapped at any time without republishing the npm package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
